questions.question: What's the actual impact of fees and slippage on retail-size paper-traded crypto strategies?
questions.summary: ~$1/round-trip on $200 positions (0.25% taker both legs). Gross +$1,085 became net -$782 on the 3-month backtest — the delta is the answer. Fees erase thin edges.

questions.question: Which parameter ranges of Bollinger and MA-crossover are robust to regime shifts, and which are overfit?
questions.summary: None are robust: all 12 variants with n>100 negative over 6 months net of costs. Per-regime conditioning untested (rung 3).

questions.question: (Emergent) Do constraints or strategies determine outcomes at this scale?
questions.summary: Constraints dominate: 99.6% of candidate signals died on cooldown/exposure/concurrency. Candidate fitness = edge per constraint slot, not signal frequency.

questions.question: Does sentiment data (Fear & Greed) improve technical strategies, or is it noise?
questions.summary: Layer 2 (context) never went live; deferred past Phase 1b per one-factor-at-a-time.

questions.question: Does the walk-forward tuner produce variants that hold up out-of-sample?
questions.summary: tune.py never built; compare.py (the scoring instrument) now exists. Blocked on the candidate roster accumulating live trades.

learnings.claim: GitHub Actions free-tier */5 cron delivers only ~3-5% of scheduled invocations on small/inactive public repos, making the 95% uptime gate unachievable there.
learnings.evidence: 2026-05-23 — Migrating cron from GitHub Actions to Hetzner VPS (69 of 1,712 expected = 4.0% invocation rate; diagnostic showed GitHub delaying/dropping triggers)

learnings.claim: The Hetzner VPS migration reliably satisfies the 95% uptime architectural gate (Phase 2 gate 1) with no operator attention.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 — 47-day operator gap; VPS ran clean (11,490+ fetch commits, zero gaps >1h, runs 84→11,574 all status='ok'; 95% uptime gate mechanically satisfied)

learnings.claim: The infrastructure/monitoring ran flawlessly but the research curriculum did not advance — 'the infrastructure was the project' — because a monitoring system with nobody reading it does not substitute for the operator showing up.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 — 47-day operator gap (signals/trades/decisions/llm_calls all still 0; STRATEGY_VARIANTS still {}; no Friday review since W21); confirms the 2026-05-23 bear case

learnings.claim: Elapsed calendar time contributes almost nothing to learning: nearly everything the project learned was produced in ~48 hours of 2026-07-16/17, and eleven weeks of calendar bought only one day of the incompressible resource (live trade-days).
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 — Correction: the sequence was valuable; the duration was not

learnings.claim: Both base strategies (Bollinger Band mean-reversion and MA-crossover) fail to clear the bar: every variant with a real sample (n>100) is net-negative over a realistic 6-month window.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 — Retire Bollinger and MA-crossover (12 variants, 1,371 trades, net −$1,719.44 / −0.13%; bollinger_default worst at n=539, −$636.75; only positives are n=3 and n=13 noise)

learnings.claim: The shared position-ceiling architecture works as specified: adding variants changes total P&L only through selection within the cap, not through added exposure.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 — Front-loaded all 12 variants on Day 1 (99.6% of candidate signals rejected; total exposure never breached)

learnings.claim: All five LLM-surfaced candidate strategies lost money net of fees over the 180-day constrained replay; none earned promotion.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 — Gauntlet results: every candidate negative net of fees (weekend −$0.21/n=79, volume_thrust −$0.63/n=38, dead_zone −$0.88/n=13, btc_leads −$1.76/n=3, liquidation −$2.56/n=4)

learnings.claim: The fee-floor finding replicated on novel strategies: weekend_illiquidity_momentum is gross-positive (win rate 49.4%, gross Sharpe 1.51) but fee-negative — its real edge is smaller than the ~0.6% round-trip cost.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 — Gauntlet results: every candidate negative net of fees

learnings.claim: The cost lever (maker fills) works mechanically: maker beat taker in 12/12 matched pairs, mean +$0.14/slot, with near-zero fill loss (0-4 of ~150 trades) because the strategy buys drawdowns into resting limits.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 — First net-positive cell; cost lever validated (exit-grid sweep of drawdown_regime_contrarian_gate)

learnings.claim: The project's first net-positive backtest cell (maker·72h·tp8%/sl4% = +$0.031/slot over 146 trades) has a statistically-zero edge — far inside noise and the best of 24 cells, so multiple-comparisons bias implies likely-negative true expectation.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 — First net-positive cell; cost lever validated; holdout NOT burned

learnings.claim: The gauntlet's fixed-n kill bar (+0.3% at n=7) has a 21% false-positive rate under pure noise, quantitatively vindicating the n<30 quarantine rule.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 — Power calibration (scripts/power_calibration.py; reports/power-calibration-2026-07-18.json)

learnings.claim: The gauntlet has near-zero detection power where interesting edges live: a true +0.17%-net edge passes only 32% at n=100 (and less with more data as the bar exceeds its true mean); reliable detection starts only ~+0.6% net.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 — Power calibration: the instrument measured itself

learnings.claim: The meta-conclusion '5-min OHLCV has no edge' is only supported as 'no edge ≥ ~0.6% net detectable by this instrument'; smaller edges remain open, answerable only by the combination stage or the live channel.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 — Power calibration: the instrument measured itself (decision c)

learnings.claim: The blind-elicitation exercise produced more validated learning in one day than any week of inside work (ceiling study, power calibration, netting insight, slow-band prior, cost decomposition all trace to it).
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 — Outside eyes institutionalized: fresh context is a standing resource

learnings.claim: The crypto cost floor is per-symbol and partly a choice: Alpaca spreads run 0.07% (BTC) to 0.42% (SOL) vs Binance ≤0.015%, so fees+spread are selectable and only slippage requires real trades to measure.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 — Critique-driven reorder (item 4; first venue measurements)

learnings.claim: The kill-criterion v1 draft was defective — it treated cost as a constant when it is a decision variable, was circular (never consulted the power study), used an AND-conjunction giving a survival dial, had a units error comparing per-round-trip cost to per-horizon edge without turnover, and had no clock or restart rule.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 — Critique-driven reorder (item 5, adversarial review findings); ratified as superseded 2026-07-19

learnings.claim: Short-horizon crypto continuation (≤24h, taker) has gross edge below the cost floor at every horizon ≤12h and is archived on the ceiling-study evidence.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-20 — K1 adjudication: short-horizon crypto archived

learnings.claim: Crypto momentum in 2024-26 is negative/reverting (0/9 cells, −12.4% directional) and equity time-series momentum also lost to drift in-window (0/9 long-or-flat vs-drift cells).
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-20 — The equity sky check, sober reading

learnings.claim: The cross-sectional 6-1 equity momentum spread is the only persistently positive signal (+sign in all four windows) but is only weakly significant (single-window t≈1; pooled t≈1.9), with survivorship inflating both figures as upper bounds.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-20 — The equity sky check, sober reading

learnings.claim: Survivorship correction weakened the cross-sectional 6-1 equity spread: 2020-21 flipped to −2.57%/mo (t−1.09), 2023 collapsed to +0.35, pooled fell to +1.25%±1.23/mo (t=+1.02, n=43) — a clean survivorship tell.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-20 — Corrected equity re-score (report equity-sky-pit-2026-07-20.json)

learnings.claim: Survivorship bias in the equity archive is fixable at zero data cost because Alpaca serves full daily bars for delisted names (SIVB, FRC, SBNY, BBBYQ, MULN all returned).
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-20 — B2 corrected-archive registration

learnings.claim: The equity pipeline is validated: round-zero 12-1 momentum was detected with the right sign and the textbook Daniel-Moskowitz momentum-crash signature (−17.3%/yr in the 2020-21 rebound; +28.9%/yr in 2024-26), strong evidence the machinery is real not a harness bug.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-20 — E2 round zero: pipeline VALIDATED (report; +14.9%/yr pooled long-only excess)

learnings.claim: The round-zero +14.9%/yr magnitude is NOT a deployable edge — far above the pre-registered 2-4%/yr band, only marginally significant (t=1.57, n=59), inflated by 2024-26 concentration, possible delisting-haircut asymmetry, and high cross-regime variance.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-20 — E2 round zero: pipeline VALIDATED (magnitude distrusted)

learnings.claim: The round-zero control was a modified pass, not the registered-power pass: the pre-registration specified 20+ years of monthly data but Alpaca history reaches only ~2021, so it ran on n=59 months — a t≈1.6 on 5 years is much weaker than the registered design.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-20 — E2 round zero: pipeline VALIDATED (AMENDMENTS, referee)

learnings.claim: The 59-month equity test window is typical, not a freak regime: French UMD 2021-26 was +0.47%/mo vs +0.62%/mo over 1927-present (z=−0.26), ruling out the 'lucked into an outlier window' failure mode.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-20 — UMD cross-check result (report umd-crosscheck-2026-07-20.json)

learnings.claim: The equity pipeline's construction is only partially cross-validated: its monthly excess correlates just +0.26 with French UMD (~7% shared variance), confirming it measures something momentum-adjacent, not that it replicates UMD's portfolio.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-20 — UMD cross-check result (yellow flag)

learnings.claim: The identity of the measured object is momentum: tilt-vs-UMD correlation rises to +0.49 in high-|UMD| storm months vs +0.09 in calm months (the 'same beast in storms' pattern), and a VW long-short 2×3 UMD mimic correlates +0.39.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-20 — Identity test result (report umd-identity-2026-07-20.json)

learnings.claim: The .env exposure incident: only the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY was displayed in a May 2026 Claude Code session transcript (Alpaca keys unexposed), and a full git-history search confirmed no real credential was ever committed to the repo.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 — Post-incident record: .env exposure (git-history search 2026-07-16)

learnings.claim: Logging incidents late costs the facts: the .env postmortem written ~60 days late lost the details that would matter (exact date, echoing command, transcript persistence), a cost the ten-minute same-day write-up would have avoided.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 — Post-incident record: .env exposure (Lesson 1)

learnings.claim: Growing synthesis LENSES from 5 to 8 silently pushed output past max_tokens, and the truncated tool input validated as {} and died with a misleading 'field missing' error.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 — Seam closed for real (Bug found and hardened against)

learnings.claim: Determinism (hash-based signal roll plus a signals-table UNIQUE constraint) is required for the null baseline: random() would double-signal on re-runs and make backtests unreproducible.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 — Null variant live (Determinism matters)

learnings.claim: Layer 4 exits are required for the placebo loop: without stop/target/24h-time exits the 5-position cap deadlocks the loop after 5 trades.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 — Null variant live (Layer 4 exits built alongside)

learnings.claim: The claude.ai Gmail connector is draft-only (cannot send), and Hetzner blocks outbound port 465 but leaves 587 open, so digest email uses SMTP via 587/STARTTLS.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 — Seam closed for real (Digest email discoveries; delivery confirmed)

learnings.claim: The prize in signal combination is netting (disagreeing signals → no trade, so cost is never paid), not √breadth diversification, and the netting ratio is computable offline from persisted trade records before any machinery is built.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 — Critique-driven reorder (item 2)

learnings.claim: The E0 fetch uses Alpaca adjustment='all' (split + dividend), so bars are total-return-adjusted and momentum ranks are valid.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-20 — B2 corrected-archive registration (Adjustment confirmed)

hypotheses.commitment: By end of Week 2, the Claude-mediated synthesis will produce either (a) a defensible justification for keeping Bollinger and MA-crossover naming which modern-filter criteria they pass/fail, or (b) a defensible alternative roster with the same justification structure; if neither, the LLM-as-epistemological-backbone thesis is weaker than expected and Week 3 starts a postmortem on the reframe.
hypotheses.resolution: Resolved by the 2026-07-02 retire-Bollinger-and-MA-crossover entry, which retired both base strategies on 6-month evidence (option b: defensible alternative roster deferred to LLM-surfaced candidates). The falsifiable hypothesis committed at the reframe was honored.

hypotheses.commitment: By end of the first calendar week of cron operation, the runs table should contain ~2,016 rows with status='ok' (90%+ success rate); if success rate is below 90%, investigate before Week 2 strategy-roster review begins.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-05-17 — Curriculum anchor is first cron run; cron wire-up; SQLite-in-repo as data store
hypotheses.resolution: Resolved against by the 2026-05-18 entry: observed cadence was ~60 min not 5 min (~24 runs/day, ~8% of assumed), far below the 90%+/2,016-row target; the hypothesis was amended and eventually the platform was migrated (2026-05-23).

hypotheses.commitment: By end of Week 2, commit a decision-log entry naming one of: (a) keep GH cron for fetch+signals, move execute to a real scheduler before Phase 2; (b) move signals+execute together to one scheduler now, keep fetch on GH cron; (c) move everything to one real scheduler before Week 3. If no decision is committed by end of Week 2, the scheduler issue becomes the most expensive un-made call and the Week 3 build doesn't begin until it's made.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-05-18 — GH cron cadence does not match assumed 5-min — recalibrating gate math
hypotheses.resolution: Effectively resolved by the 2026-05-23 migration decision (option c-like): migrate the cron to a Hetzner CX22 VPS, running fetch.py unchanged and disabling the GitHub Actions cron.

hypotheses.commitment: Original: 90%+ success rate by end of the first calendar week (of GitHub-Actions-invoked runs).
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-05-17 — Curriculum anchor is first cron run (amended at 2026-05-23)
hypotheses.resolution: Amended at 2026-05-23 into two separately tracked metrics (invocation rate ≥99% VPS-controlled, code success rate ≥95% project-controlled; combined 95%+ uptime of expected runs over any 4-week window) to remove the platform-induced ceiling that made the original unfalsifiable.

hypotheses.commitment: The residual crypto program (24h+maker fills and reversion-shaped 2-8 week specs) remains ALIVE under kill-criterion v2, resolving by 2026-08-31, gated on the real-money RTC measurement; if the RTC cannot run by 2026-08-15, the residual resolves on the assumed 0.6% cost figure treated as adverse.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-07-20 — K1 adjudication: short-horizon crypto archived, residual on a dated clock

hypotheses.commitment: By 2026-08-31, and every 6 months thereafter, run the ceiling study per a locked analysis plan; if the one-sided 90% upper confidence bound of net_edge is below zero at every horizon in H (with CI method validated against the power table), a 60-day execution-cost-only remediation window opens, and if breakeven RTC is still unachievable, program v1 archives per Phase-1b terms.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-07-19 — Operator ratification: all pending drafts approved (kill criterion v2)

hypotheses.commitment: Round-zero pre-registration: the positive control is cross-sectional 12-1 momentum, long-only US large-cap tilt; DETECTION = landing inside or above the literature band (~2-4%/yr over benchmark, post-2000, decaying) with the sign right, at the significance 20+ years of monthly data affords. A noisy positive is NOT detection.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-07-20 — The equity sky check, sober reading (round-zero pre-registration)
hypotheses.resolution: Round zero detected 12-1 momentum with the right sign and the textbook crash signature (2026-07-20 E2 round zero entry). Pipeline validated for research use — though as a modified n=59 pass, not the registered 20-year-power pass, and the +14.9%/yr magnitude was explicitly not treated as detection of a deployable edge.

hypotheses.commitment: Pre-commitment (decided before corrected numbers exist): if the corrected re-score flips the cross-sectional sign or collapses pooled t toward zero, the E2 rationale formally reverts to breadth-and-throughput-only, and round zero proceeds ANYWAY.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-07-20 — The equity sky check, sober reading (B2 corrected-archive registration)
hypotheses.resolution: Fired as pre-committed: the 2026-07-20 corrected re-score collapsed pooled t to +1.02 and flipped the 2020-21 window sign, so the E2 rationale reverted to breadth-and-throughput-only and round zero proceeded regardless — no improvised decision required.

hypotheses.commitment: Equity kill criterion (draft): round zero must first VALIDATE the pipeline (detect 12-1 momentum inside/above the 2-4%/yr band, correct sign); once validated, if no equity spec beats the equal-weight benchmark net of costs at a 90% upper-CB over the corrected archive with adequate power, the equity program archives.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-07-20 — E2 OPENED: equity cross-sectional program (operator GO)
hypotheses.resolution: Pipeline-validation prerequisite met (round zero validated 2026-07-20); the archive/beat-the-benchmark condition remains open pending equity research rounds.
